candidates.stmt:
John has many **years of experience** in the built environment, first working in the **construction industry** and later as a lecturer and team leader at **Northumbria University.** He has also served in a **wide range of voluntary roles,** both within the church and across various community organisations.
An active Green Party candidate for **over 20 years,** John has welcomed the party's evolution **beyond a purely environmental focus,** recognising the importance of **addressing wider social and economic challenges.**
John firmly believes that politics should not simply be "the art of the possible", but a commitment to **doing what is right.**

(April 2024:)
I’m a west end lad, a Geordie. Been living here most of my life. Went to Westgate Hill Primary and then Heaton Manor Secondary. I’m married, my wife’s a dinner lady and my son attends a local secondary school. I live locally, in an ex-Council house in a Council estate.

I’m a Town Planner by profession, having worked for both the private sector and local Councils. Before that I use to be a Community Development Worker employed at various charities including Gateshead CVS. I owned and ran a Planning Design Consultancy and a Pizza shop business.

I have served as a senior Committee Member with Newcastle Mela, the NBA and the UBYA. The same UBYA that use to be based at the former Murray House now NUcastle Foundation.

I have been a voice for the community with Spice FM presenting a weekly show for over three years discussing community issues.

I have firsthand experience recruiting people, writing strategy, implementing policies, managing budgets, and executing meaningful action.

Former Councillor, the late Nigel Todd was one of my political inspirations. One of his flagship projects was the Greening Wingrove & Arthurs Hill initiative. Both to honour his legacy and continue the good work, I pledge to continue supporting the project during my term.

I decided to become a Councillor because, like you, I’m tired of the same old Council and want to see change.

(April 2026:)

Rowshon brings over a decade of experience in the community and voluntary sector, supporting individuals facing complex challenges related to **unemployment, mental health, and overall wellbeing.** He has worked closely with local organisations to **improve access to essential services.**
He has also worked within an architectural firm, gaining insight into the **built environment and development processes,** and **held roles within planning departments at several local councils.** This gives him **practical experience** in planning policy, local development frameworks, and the operatio

Sully, a local businessman and entrepreneur, was **born and raised in Newcastle** and is proud to call the city home. He values its long-standing **diversity** and the strong sense of **community** that has brought people together across cultures and backgrounds.
He is standing for election for two reasons; firstly because the rise of the far right risks undermining decades of progress in **building inclusive, cohesive communities.** Secondly, to speak on behalf of a community frustrated by the Labour-led council's inaction, rising council tax, and growing hardship, Sully believes **it is time for residents to stand up and be counted.**

Hannah is committed to improving the lives of her local community by working with residents and building grassroots power. As a resident in Arthur’s Hill since 2023, Hannah’s priorities are your priorities; action on litter and fly-tipping, safety for women and girls, and good quality secure housing. As a private renter herself, she knows that too often housing can be unsafe or uncomfortable, and that private landlords can be unresponsive to tenants requests. As a city we must fight against the blight of poor landlords and hold them responsible, using every tool at our disposal. Hannah is an active trade unionist, and in her jobs in hospitality and on the Tyne & Wear Metro has helped fight for workers pay and conditions. She is currently a workplace rep, and works for a trade union.

Hannah says: “As a young working class woman, I can represent groups too often excluded from our local democracy. I am proud to stand as a trade unionist to further the rights of all working people.”

Hayder has been a Labour councillor in Newcastle since 2023, serving residents with dedication and a strong sense of duty. Raised in Arthur’s Hill, he attended the local schools, grew up using our parks and community centres, and knows first-hand the challenges and strengths that make this area unique. He has deep ties to our neighbourhood, having previously served on the boards of both Nunsmoor Centre Trust and Greening Wingrove, working to support families, strengthen community spaces and improve our local environment. Alongside his council work, Hayder is an NHS pharmacist and Unite workplace representative. His experience on the frontline of healthcare has shaped his commitment to protecting public services, tackling inequality and speaking up for people.

Joanne has been a councillor in this area since 2004 firstly in Westgate ward then for Arthur’s Hill following boundary changes in 2018 when she was elected to represent both the Labour Party and the Co-Operative Party. She has worked closely with local residents, council officers, police and other stakeholders to try and improve the quality of life for local people. She has also been a trustee on the Nunsmoor Centre Trust and she chairs the Corporate Parent Advisory Committee of the council so supporting and ensuring the best provision for some of our most vulnerable children and young people.

As a local resident Rob has been proud to represent his community for almost 30 years. He loves talking to residents and hearing what is important to you. His priorities are improving the environment and he carries out regular litter picks and better education by improving the quality of our schools to give our children the best possible start in life.

Susan has lived, worked and raised a family in the local area for the last 50 years, her working background is supporting those facing homelessness, domestic violence and caring roles. With a background in supporting some of our most vulnerable people she brings a strong voice and a clear understanding of the challenges facing local residents. Susan was first elected as a councillor for the ward in 2022.

I have spent 35 years working as a doctor. I'm now standing as a Green candidate to **fight for our NHS;** for **equal access** and **assured funding. The NHS should not be for sale.**
The Green Party offers our best chance to make the UK **a great place for our children to grow up.** There is a huge need to **invest in housing, education and training.**
Only the Greens offer this.
Newcastle is famous for its sense of community. If elected, **I will work for the local community and be there for you.**

I'm a local, a mum raising my family as **proud Geordies,** and an academic who has spent years **fighting for social justice.** Now, I'm standing as your Green candidate to bring that fight to our ward because **we deserve more.**
From taxing billionaires to fund our services to challenging the presence of an Israeli-state-owned factory on our doorstep - **I stand for justice, here and abroad.**
My children grew up in Benwell schools; **I know this community.** Let's trade empty promises for **real graft and Green common sense.** I'm a neighbour **ready to work for you,** it's time for **unity and action.**

I’m hearing a lot from some candidates about national and international issues.
People will always have views on those and that’s fair but this is a local election.
For me, it’s about Benwell, Scotswood and Denton Burn.
I live here, I work here, I grew up here. I see the same issues as everyone else every day.
I’m also hearing a lot of “I used to live here” or “I worked here at one point”.
For me, there’s a difference between having a past connection and actually being part of the community now.
Too often, party politics gets in the way of what residents actually need.
Let’s not make the same mistake again, just with different colours.
This should be about our community and getting things sorted properly.
If you’re thinking of supporting me, I’d really appreciate your vote 👍

I'm running in Benwell, Scotswood and Denton Burn - but whichever ward of Newcastle, or wherever you live, then vote Green to see positive change!

It is time for change and for a bold move forwards. Together we can stand against those that try and divide us. A vote for Green on the 7th May will build a positive future for Benwell, Scotswood & Denton Burn, for wider Newcastle, and also nationally - sending a strong message of hope to all corners of our country.

I have lived and worked in the West End of Newcastle for over a decade. It often feels like the West End is overlooked and under supported - let's change that together. From a variety of community projects I have run in the area over the last several years, I know first hand how important green spaces and opportunities for young people are to residents. I will fight for better youth and social services, cleaner streets and parks, and support local businesses to thrive.

Vote Green on the 7th May for a cleaner, more united Benwell, Scotswood & Denton Burn with a bright future.

Born and raised in the Boundary of Newcastle upon Tyne Central and West

Legal Kinship Guardian for niece

Working class local care worker

Passionate about local issues that affect our families

Juna Sathian is the sitting councillor for Blakelaw and Cowgate since 2022, working consistently for local residents with a hands-on, visible approach. She is known for being on top of casework, responding quickly and following issues through to resolution. She carries out regular walkabouts, home visits, and works closely with council teams, housing officers and police to tackle issues such as waste, housing repairs, parking and anti-social behaviour. Juna lives in Blakelaw, and her sons attend a local primary school, giving her a strong personal connection to the area. In 2021, she founded Clean Community, Clean World, a weekly litter-picking initiative bringing together families, schools and residents. Alongside her councillor role, Juna is an award-winning scientist and Associate Professor at Northumbria University, Newcastle. Known for being approachable and responsive, she focuses on practical action and getting results. She continues working with residents to make Blakelaw, Cowgate and North Fenham cleaner, greener and safer.

Karen was born and bred in Newcastle, and lives locally with her family. She has served as a councillor since 2014 and has led the council since October 2024. If re-elected she will continue to work hard on your behalf, and will always take time to listen to you and act on what you tell her matters most. Before becoming a councillor Karen worked across several roles including in the social housing sector, with communities and in regeneration. These experiences helped shape her priorities, which include high-quality safe and secure homes, maintaining clean and welcoming neighbourhoods, keeping our communities safe, supporting families and giving every child the best possible start in life.

Ian has lived in Newcastle for many years and his family grew up here.  He has a lot of experience of working with people in communities and in their jobs to resolve issues and get things done.  He is committed to involving and co-operating with local people, community groups and local services to help make Blakelaw and Cowgate and the wider city a better place to live and work.

Having lived all over the United Kingdom, I settled in Newcastle 15 years ago to start my family, and I wouldn't want to live anywhere else.
Newcastle is filled with **friendly, hard working, family focused communities. These proud communities** have been neglected Chrough funding cuts in recent years, which has caused an increase in anti social behaviour and vandalism. These problems are compounded by a lack of resources along with the cost of living crisis.
I want to see an **increase in funding to the outer wards of Newcastle.** I want to see an **increase in amenities for children, teenagers and families.** I want our children to have access to **a more positive, green future.** Access to a **good education** and a **properly funded Special Educational Needs programs.**
We deserve **a cleaner city** with **more green energy, more local investment** and **more work opportunities, family support and youth projects.**

I'm Tina lon, a Green Party candidate rooted in Blakelaw and Cowgate since 2018, committed to building a community where people **look out for one another** and **feel they truly belong.** My work has focused on **inclusion, mutual support,** and standing up for **those whose voices are too often unheard.**
I believe social justice must be active, not symbolic. That means **challenging discrimination, supporting marginalised communities,** and **speaking clearly against injustice** in all its forms. I stand **firmly against genocide** and **for human rights,** and I reject attempts to conflate legitimate criticism of injustice with antisemitism-because **protecting both human dignity and truth matters.**
My priorities are simple: **safer streets, better green spaces, and stronger community connections.** I want a future shaped by **unity, fairness, and democratic participation,** where **residents have a real say and no one is left behind.**

I've lived in Newcastle since 1987, and am a long-term member of the Green Party. I worked in Cowgate several years back; I am heartened when **communities pull together** and **advocate for what is needed locally,** rather than be divided.
We want **better waste management** and **cleaner streets; access to green space which works for wildlife** and for **safe play space for kids; bring empty buildings back into good use.**
I'm encouraged by **what Green Party Councillors are already managing in Newcastle City Council** and despite times of austerity want to help bring about **positive change.**

I became Byker’s first elected Green councillor in 2024. Since then, I’ve focused on turning conversations into real wins for our community. We worked with residents to challenge unfair District Heating costs and secured lower bills for homeowners. The Byker Clean Streets Campaign has brought people together, building pride in our area through regular community action. Work with local charities is helping lay the groundwork for projects that tackle anti-social behaviour and create new opportunities across Byker.

My day job as a Clinical Psychologist is all about helping people with change. Look at the state of politics. It’s clear that change is needed.

At a council level, Greens have achieved changes to the budget, supporting people in the poorest parts of our city. We’ve shown that politics can work differently when it’s rooted in the community. Let’s keep going.

Over the 10 years that I've lived in Byker, I have been hoping to see councillors who would understand the challenges we have with anti-social behaviour and housing standards. The Greens are working hard on this and I want to help them do more.

I believe that the Green Party's values on property standards and community policing, which focus on increasing social housing, investing in affordable local transport and reversing cuts to police budgets - would make a huge difference to the lives of people living in Byker and Newcastle.

That is why I am standing as a local councillor on May 7th for Byker ward, to help make Byker a safer, cleaner and more affordable place for both residents and businesses to live and thrive!

I have been active in Byker since moving in 2010, and really love the lively, friendly community I have found here. Our neighbours welcomed us and made us feel at home and I should like to repay their kindness. I am a member of Byker Mutual Aid (volunteering to fight food poverty and support our neighbours) and Byker Community Planting, (connecting gardeners across Byker), running art and gardening workshops for these. Also, I regularly volunteered with Byker Community Centre until its closure and am keen to support its replacement.

My main motivation for standing is to make change for the better in my community. Locally, this could mean: a focus on food poverty; housing support and advice for tenants and residents; campaigning for cheaper, more regular public transport; and continuing improvements to refuse collection and anti fly-tipping strategies, for a cleaner, safer neighbourhood.

I was honoured to be asked to work with Nick, and Jamie Anderson, in Byker making the Green Party visible, accountable and effective. Clearly, we can have a more powerful impact in our area if voted in.

Gavin is a husband and father of two with strong family and community values. He works in adult social care, where he has developed extensive experience managing complex caseloads and advocating for service users and residents. His career has focused on supporting people, navigating complex systems, and ensuring individuals receive the services and support they are entitled to. Gavin cares deeply about our communities and the issues that affect everyday life, he believes in open dialogue, listening to different perspectives, and working toward practical solutions that strengthen the places we live.

Khadija is a proud student at Northumbria University and a young person who believes that our community deserves positive leadership, opportunity, and unity. She is standing as a local councillor to give back to the city that has shaped her education and future. She understands the pressures facing students, families, and working people, from housing and safety to jobs and inclusion. She wants to help build a community where everyone feels heard, supported, and respected.

Will is standing for election in Byker because he cares deeply about supporting our community and has a passion for helping those in need. He is committed to listening, working hard and standing up for the people of Byker and the issues they are facing.

I am standing for election because our community deserves **fairness, resilience, and accountability.** As Chair of the GPNA and a school leader, I've spent years on the front lines **challenging big developers and the Council,** watching vital infrastructure, like our GP surgery and bus links, be sidelined for profit. I want to bring this **street-level experience** to the Civic Centre.
You should pick me because I am a **local champion** who is **already doing the work.** From fighting for **safer roads** to **tackling "nightmare" transport,** I don't wait for an election to show up. I don't just identify problems; I know the policy levers to fix them.
Vote Green for **a watchdog and a voice** for a completed, connected Great Park.

I am standing because our community deserves **a strong voice** that truly listens to residents and works for our **long-term good.** Through my work with the Great Park Neighbourhood Association, I have worked alongside residents **to raise local concerns, support community activities and bring people together to address issues** that matter to us all.

Like many residents, I care deeply about the future of our area. Castle Ward is a fantastic place to live, with vibrant communities, beautiful green spaces and families who want the very best for their neighbourhood. However, many residents feel their concerns are not always properly heard. Local decisions can sometimes feel distant from the people they affect most. That is why I have decided to stand for election.

I am not a career politician. I am someone **already working** alongside residents to improve our neighbourhood. If elected, I will represent Castle Ward with **integrity, energy, and commitment.** I would be grateful for your support.

My name is Andrew, and I am standing as a Green Party candidate because **I can no longer be complacent** in the face of the rise of the far right and the negligence of the established political parties. **I will do what is right simply because it is right.**
I spent a decade as a secondary **school science teacher** in the most deprived areas of the North East. Now, as a senior **financial crime consultant** for one of the world's largest audit firms and a regular food bank volunteer, I want to bring my commitment to **transparency and evidence-based solutions** to our ward.
I support the Green Party because they align with my values and are the only party **brave enough to openly stand up for common decency** in the face of the hatred stirred up by those seeking power in Westminster. I would be honoured to earn your support.

Gary was born and raised in County Durham and has lived in Neville’s Cross for nearly 22 years. He is a Professor of Protein Biochemistry and Head of the Department of Applied Sciences at Northumbria University, and is a founding member of a North East based enzyme manufacturing company.

Andrew has lived in Castle ward for 8 years and has been a councillor for the ward since May 2024. He has worked for the NHS in a few different roles since 2013. Most recently, he has spent 7 years working in the team that run clinical trials for cancer patients at the Freeman. He is very community minded and always gets stuck in with community event. The thing Andrew likes best about the ward is the community spirit that can be found in all parts of the ward.

Stephen has served as a city councillor for the last fourteen years. His first priority will be to the people of Castle Ward, Great Park and the villages that make up our part of Newcastle. As part of your local Labour team, he has been involved in many projects; supporting community groups and dealing with resident’s problems and concerns. He also works as a teacher of children with special needs so education and young people are high on his list of priorities.

Mark has loved in the local community most of his life and grew up in the area. His father is the well known and respected local parish priest for Brunswick and Hazlerigg. White at university, Mark was a volunteer at the Citizens Advice Bureau, and has a desire to help local people and stand up for their rights. He currently works for the NHS in a patient facing administrative role – helping patients with queries.

Dave Cook is a retired Lecturer in Medical Science, and a former School Governor. He served as a Councillor for the Lemington ward for 12 years, and has lived in the Outer West for over 50 years so knows what it takes to be a councillor and local champion.  Although not born in Newcastle, he is an adopted Geordie with a Geordie family. Dave is President of a Local Cricket Club, and an administrator in the local cricket league. He has a keen interest and involvement with grass-roots sport, having played football and hockey with local teams when younger. After retirement he continued as a hockey umpire.

Owen was born in Newcastle, studied in the city and has played an active role in local life, including volunteering as a Scout Leader and supporting community events. Community has always mattered to Owen, whether it is through volunteering or getting involved in local projects. He believes that showing up, listening and working hard to make a difference matters.

I think local politics should serve local communities. Things need to change for Newcastle.

I am approachable, hard working, conscientious - qualities essential for serving the Chapel community. I've lived here for almost 15 years, and work for the NHS as a Clinical Psychologist.

Local councillors should have the needs of the Chapel community at heart. As your Green councillor, I'm the right person for the job.

Clifford for Change in May

Ben works at Jesmond Park Academy as a member of the school support staff and is also studying for a Physics degree, giving him a front-row view of the challenges facing young people and families in our community. He is passionate about improving local services, strengthening education, and creating real opportunities for everyone. He will champion STEM education, push for better support for neurodivergent young people, and work to make our council more responsive, fair, and forward‑thinking. His experience working in a Covid testing lab during the pandemic shaped his belief in strong public services, science and research and community resilience. Ben is committed to empowering young people, including supporting votes at 16, and ensuring every resident has a voice in shaping our future.

I want to help Dene and South Gosforth and our wider city - become a greener, safer, fairer and more prosperous place to live. Having called this area home for over a decade, I know it well, and believe it can be even better than it is today.

I'm an architect specialising in low-energy homes, both new builds and retrofits, and I'm passionate about using those skills to help drive the changes we need.

In an uncertain world, it's the Green Party that has the courage to stand up and make the right choices for our future.

For decades, Dene and South Gosforth has been held back by complacency while we're told to pay more for less it's time for bold visible change. Our council should serve local people, not private contractors and big corporations.

Using my political experience, I will fight for warmer, more affordable homes; publicly run transport; and community-owned energy that cuts bills and carbon. I'll strengthen local businesses and co-operatives -creating wealth and opportunities in our neighbourhoods - while protecting our green spaces and tackling pollution in the Ouseburn.

Together, we can build a fairer, stronger, and more prosperous Dene and South Gosforth.

Hilary has lived in Dene and South Gosforth ward for forty years, and her three children have all settled here too, and she already know many residents. Her children all went to local schools, and Hilary was a school governor for many years, and her grandchildren are currently benefitting from the good quality education available here. Hilary  was previously a councillor in other parts of the City but always wanted to represent her own ward. She has always been very happy here but as a local resident of course she knows very well what the issues are, and is very capable of standing up for our community. She will continue to hold the monthly street stall on the High Street that she has run for many years to hear what you care about, and convey it to the council and our MP Catherine McKinnell.

Alex is a student, who has lived in Dene and South Gosforth ward for all his life, and feels a deep connection to the area. Along with his wider family, he has volunteered regularly at local charity events, which he has been doing so from a young age, and feels it gave him a sense of social responsibility and desire to do good. He wants to protect local green spaces, having grown up walking around and enjoying Paddy Freeman’s Park and Jesmond Dene.

As an NHS worker and active member of UNISON, Marley sees every day how vital strong public services are, and what happens when they are underfunded. She is committed to protecting and investing in our NHS, social care, youth services, and local amenities so they truly serve our community. She has consistently campaigned for low-paid workers to be paid the real Living Wage and have worked to strengthen workplace protections, including supporting the implementation of the Employment Rights Act. As a young worker, she is passionate about improving the voice and representation of young people in decision-making. Marley believes issues such as sustainability, clean and healthy environments are fundamental fair and thriving communities. If elected, she will stand up for fair pay, fair funding for strong public services, and a council that works for everyone.

I want a council that stands up for the people of this city and this region by supporting local businesses and local people and that keeps more of our money right here in the North East through community wealth building. I also want more green and community spaces for people to enjoy without having to spend their hard earned money..

Only the Greens offer this and that's why I'm standing to be your Green Party councillor.

Politicians should serve us, not billionaires or large corporations! I will always work for you to deliver a safer, greener and more hopeful future for you and I.

I am Halimah Begum, and I am standing to represent **the community that raised me.** As a lifelong Elswick resident, wife, and mother, I do not just observe the challenges our families face; **I live them every day.**
I currently serve as a Housing Support Officer for a local council, where I witness the housing crisis firsthand. With a degree in Law and experience as a Charity Trustee, I possess the **professional expertise to fight for justice** and advocate **for those the system often overlooks.**
I will focus on the issues that impact our daily lives, from delivering **better housing** and **fixing potholes** to tackling **littering** and securing **improved transport.** I will also lobby the NHS for **shorter waiting lists** and stand as **a firm voice against the introduction of digital ID.**
I am ready to be a bridge for all generations, ensuring **Elswick is always a priority.** I am your **neighbour and your voice.**

As a **Green councillor** and **Newcastle's first councillor of Bangladeshi origin**, Khaled has spent the past two years working closely with residents across Elswick - listening, supporting, and **taking action on the issues that matter most.** From **tackling fly-tipping** and **improving local parks** to **supporting community-led initiatives** and **strengthening neighbourhood services**, his focus has always been on **practical change that people can see and feel.**
With a strong commitment to **social inclusion, climate action and community engagement,** Khaled has **led local projects**, supported **digital inclusion**, and worked alongside volunteers and organisations to build a fairer, **cleaner and more connected community.** He is committed to continuing this work and serving the people of Elswick with **integrity and dedication.**

Peter Thomson has lived in Elswick for over 40 years as an **active member of the community, a school governor** at Canning Street Primary for nearly 50 years, and a **Trustee of Newcastle's West End Children's Community.**
He **represented Elswick on the City Council** for 20 years before Labour lost its way. For 10 years now he has been a member of the Green Party actively organising to improve the streets by **tackling fly-tipping and rubbish building up in back lanes;** lobbying for **road improvements** and **improved public transport.**
Elswick is a wonderful, **diverse community,** with **great potential and opportunity.** Peter is dedicated to working and supporting the people of Elswick to achieve that potential and those opportunities.

Mohammed is a community activist and qualified food safety trainer based in Elswick,. He plays an active role in strengthening the community and supporting diaspora development initiatives. He has reintroduced Bangla School, raised funds for Palestine, flood victims of Bangladesh and Pakistan, earthquake victims of Turkey. He is General Secretary of Newcastle Bangladeshi Association and since 2018, he has held the position of Joint Secretary of the Greater Sylhet Development and Welfare Council UK, contributing to welfare, education, and community link programmes between the UK and Bangladesh.

Margaret is a Specialist Community Nurse, and University lecturer, she has worked in Elswick for 25 years, supporting the community with health initiatives and providing a nursing service. She has had the experience of serving as a city councillor in Newcastle in the early 2000’s. During this time she used her public health expertise to serve on the Health and Social Care Committee As a qualified University lecturer she taught pre and post graduate students Public Health. She has a vast experience of community services within the city and in Elswick.

I, Nicu Ion, am committed to building a future where every community is empowered, every voice is heard, and every individual has access to equal opportunities. Together, we can strengthen the foundations of democracy and uphold the rule of law, ensuring fairness and justice for all. My vision is for a society where prosperity is shared, and thriving businesses drive sustainable growth while creating opportunities for everyone. By fostering unity, celebrating diversity, and championing inclusivity, I aim to create a brighter, more equitable future where everyone has the tools to succeed and contribute to a better tomorrow. Join me in this mission to empower our communities and achieve progress for all.
For our communities, for our city, for our future Putting community at the heart of politics Focusing on the issues that matter to the people of Newcastle Taking climate change seriously.

I moved to Newcastle in 2020 at the start of the COVID-19 pandemic, and the warmth and community I found here has stayed with me ever since. This city has become my home, because of its people.

I believe politics can and should be hopeful, and that change starts locally. That's why I'm standing as a Green councillor for Fawdon & West Gosforth.

I want to stand up for our community - tackling the issues that matter the most to residents, whether improving local services, protecting green spaces, or promoting equality and safety.

Newcastle deserves leadership that listens, cares and acts. I'm ready to play my part in making our city a fairer, greener, more hopeful place to live.

Hello, I'm Isabella, a 21 year old community theatre student from Walker. I am passionate about social justice, especially to do with class and minorities. I have specialties in community and outreach through my degree and am passionate about making politics an accessible, warm space that everyone, regardless of background, can access and feel a part of.

Growing up queer, I am very aware of how important it is to see people who represent you in positions of power which is one of the main reasons I wanted to stand. As a young queer woman, I don't often see people who look like me in political positions so I decided to become the voice I always looked for growing up. I will always stand for equality, empathy and love and hope to bring those ideals into your council because we all deserve to feel safe, listened to and supported in our communities.

I have lived in Newcastle all my adult life and as an experienced NHS doctor, I've witnessed the NHS increasingly struggle to provide quality healthcare for all due to the actions of people who place their own personal gain over everyone and everything else. All public services and the UK population have suffered similarly.

The people of Newcastle and the Green Party believe in fairness. We'll work hard to provide affordable living, great employment opportunities, attentive public services, better transport options and shared public spaces for us all to enjoy. We will ensure city contracts go to responsible local businesses and communities to support local people rather than going offshore to tax havens.

We achieve all this by working together and not by blaming the most vulnerable in society. Your vote for the Green Party is a vote for hope in a new beginning for our city and our country.

I came to the UK in 1978, within MBBS degree. Soon acquired and MRCGP title, did the full vocational training for general practice and stayed in one practice as a senior partner from 1983 until 2014.  Worked for a decade in community radiowww.spicefm.Co.uk, wrote books on stress,.music in schools and promoted complimentary therapies. Joined the Conservative party in July 2018, stood as a candidate in Gosforth in May 2021 and got 1152 votes. I have worked every single day since then and intend to make Gosforth clean green Township with thriving business and zero tolerance to crime. Help me achieve this. Available 24/7 +447714268168

Tom was born in Byker, and went into the NHS nurse training at the age of 23. He initially worked to get young people and children into the community instead of long stay hospitals, and was one of the first leaning disability community nurses in Northumberland.  Tom was awarded the MBE in 2010 for services to nursing and to learning disabilities services. He has sat for several years on the Chief constables independent advisory group, is a public governor for both the Northumberland NHS trust and currently the Newcastle hospitals NHS trust. Tom also volunteers at St Mary’s Cathedral, and is a volunteer litter picker around the City.

I'm standing as a Green Party candidate in Gosforth because I believe our community deserves better. For too long, we've seen governments and councils fall short, with cuts to public services, rising living costs and little forward planning for future generations.

We need practical, progressive local leadership that focuses on the issues people face every day, whilst sending a clear message that there is an alternative to the current political system and Reform's politics of fear and division.

I'm an honorary Geordie who moved here permanently post-Covid, Gosforth became home to me and my teenage son. I care deeply about the quality of our schools, affordable housing, improving our public transport system and creating opportunities for young people.

As your councillor, I will listen and act to deliver a greener, fairer future for Gosforth.

Jonathan is standing to deliver practical, community-led change and a stronger local voice at City Hall. A family man with deep roots in the area, Jonathan understands the everyday priorities that matter most to residents; safe streets, strong schools and reliable local services. He brings senior leadership experience in communications, public engagement and organisational strategy, with a proven track record of working with diverse stakeholders to get results. Jonathan is also deeply committed to public service in Newcastle through local governance and community leadership, supporting schools and neighbourhood initiatives. He will be a visible, responsive councillor who listens first and acts fast.

Colin has lived in Newcastle since birth, he is married with three children and six grandchildren. By profession he worked in the Community Care Alarms Service and was an active member of the GMB trade union. He has been retired for the last 16 years, and wants to use his time to fight for the people of Newcastle.

John a retired Chief Inspector with 30 years of policing experience, will bring expertise in local engagement and community safety. He is committec to serving Gosforth, its residents, aims to address local issues and preserve the area's unique character.

George has lived in Gosforth for nearly 30 years and as a successful entrepreneur and BBC journalist, he is committed to practical solutions, accountability, and putting your community first. Together, we can make our area a better place to live, work, and thrive.

I’m not a career politician, and I haven’t spent my life in politics. I’ve spent it working in business, solving problems, managing teams, and being accountable for results. Over time, like many people, I’ve found myself increasingly concerned about the direction things are going and the impact that is having at a local level.
I’ve also faced my own personal health challenges in recent years, which has given me a very real understanding of how much people rely on services working properly and being there when they are needed.
Like many of you, I care about where I live. I want Gosforth to be a place where services are reliable, streets are well looked after, and people feel safe in their own community.
Speaking to residents across the area, a few things come up time and again:
• People want to be listened to  
• They want to see where their money is going  
• They want to feel safe in their homes and neighbourhoods  
• And they want action — not just promises  
That’s exactly what I stand for.
Clear, accountable spending.  
Better, more consistent local services.  
And a councillor who is present, listens, and follows things through — with a focus on delivery, not just promises.
I’ll be out across Gosforth in the coming weeks speaking to as many people as I can. If there’s something affecting your street or your area, please feel free to contact me directly.

For many years, local communities have been underserved by mainstream parties whose priorities are too often shaped by national agendas, pushing the priorities of their national leadership before local people.

Over the last decade and under the current leadership, we’ve seen public spaces disappear, green areas become harder to access, and roads left in a cycle of temporary fixes despite continued objections and campaigning from the Green Party.

We need more than small touch-ups to street benches and planters. We need real care, real attention, and people who will truly stand up for each and every single one of their consistituents.
We believe our community deserves better than short-term fixes and top-down decisions. It deserves representatives who listen, show up, and get things done.

Where we’ve been elected, that’s exactly what we’ve done—working with residents, keeping our wards clean, and standing up to serve our constituents.

If you want a council that is responsive, visible, and committed to improving everyday life, a council which promotes ethical investments, do your part and vote for hope in the May elections.

Andrew has been at the forefront of the Green Party in Heaton for over a decade. Andrew is a passionate advocate for human rights, a defender of the working class dedicating his life to better living standards for his ward and an advocate for a stronger, thriving more cohesive community for Heaton residents!

With Andrew's leadership he has brought the Greens Party in at second place in Heaton for over 10 years! With your help, that's all about to change on 7th May ✊🏾

Jo - an activist and brilliant public speaker says "we are campaigning to be your Councillors because we understand the privilege of the role and what it demands. We understand our obligations to fight for every single one of you".

SAVE OUR PARKS AND GREEN SPACES - with a specific plan to improve and protect ALL across Newcastle, including Heaton and Armstrong Parks and Iris Brickfield.

ACTION ON HEATON'S STREETS - We'll make back lanes, graffiti, litter bins, and tackling road and pavement defects a priority again.

PROTECTING RENTERS FROM BAD LANDLORDS - We have many good small landlords, but we need better regulation of big private landlords and action on council property maintenance.

Liberal Democrats have represented North Jesmond Ward for some 21 years and have built a strong reputation for taking up the issues that matter to local people, and keeping in regular touch through the Focus newsletter and through the emailed eFocus. Councillor Gerry Keating leads the team, supported by Philip Browne, with assistance from former North Jesmond councillors Peter Breakey, Catherine Walker, Ron Armstrong, Lucy Keating, Deborah Burns and Libby Dicken.

Philip Browne will be offereing himslef as the Liberal Democrats candidate at the election on Thursday 5th May 2022

I'm a social and economic researcher at an employee-owned company in Newcastle, and I'm standing in Jesmond because I want to fight for a local economy that works better for local people. Too often, money flows out of our communities to big corporations while local people, shops and services lose out.

My professional experience means I understand how decisions are made and how often communities are overlooked. I also want to improve women's representation on the council, because local politics should better reflect the people it serves.

Greens are the first choice in this election for a fairer local economy where communities come first.

I've lived and worked around Newcastle for over 30 years, as a GP, generally in deprived communities. It's angered me how government policies, Tory and now 'Labour' have cut social facilities while channelling money to the rich and to large corporations. I am appalled by the state of public transport and the privatised water companies dumping raw sewage into rivers.

I joined the Green Party as it's the only party fighting for a fairer society, and for protection of the climate and wider environment that is being dangerously polluted and damaged by big business. And because it wants to make our government fairer, and refuses money from rich lobbyists.

As a Green councillor for Jesmond, I will support better public transport, regulation of landlords and HMO's, more consultation with residents, and better upkeep of our open spaces. The constant decline of Jesmond Dene is a disgrace areas like this are important 'urban lungs' for so many people.

I am Ludovico Rella, I am an Assistant Professor at Durham University researching the impact of Artificial Intelligence on society. I moved to the North East from Italy ten years ago anc I fell in love with Newcastle, its history, its culture and its values.

I believe in public, affordable and accessible higher education and in a sustainable and constructive relationship between universities and the local community. I want to contribute to bringing students and local residents together in Jesmond to build a stronger and more inclusive community, and to ensure affordability and quality of rental properties.

I stand as a Green candidate not only to make sure that divisive and hateful politics stay out of power in Newcastle, but also to contribute making our city a positive example for progressive, equitable and sustainable policies that can inspire the rest of the UK.

Killian has been a resident of Jesmond for Nearly 20 years and cares deeply about the area. From supporting our nature resources in the wonderful Jesmond Dene to the Town Moor. Building and maintaining relationships with the Freemen of Newcastle and continuing to protect Jesmond Dene are essential. As a teacher for 20 years, he understand the need to build positive partnerships with residents and stakeholders. As a Run Director at Town Moor parkrun, he sees thousands of residents and tourists come visit us in Jesmond and is proud to showcase and promote our city and Jesmond itself. Killian wants to protect residents’ streets as well – with quick resolution of potholes and litter in our streets and back lanes, but also protecting the quality of rental housing in the area.

Finley is a Law student at Newcastle University with a strong interest in justice, public service, and community representation. He is actively involved in politics and is committed to ensuring that the voices of those living in Jesmond are properly heard. He believes both long-term residents and students deserve fair representation and a strong local voice. If elected, Finley will work hard to deliver for Jesmond and put residents at the heart of every decision. His priorities include improving housing standards, tackling the cost of living, and protecting Jesmond’s environment and community character.

John has had a varied working life, from heavy industry, Hospitality and Security, he also served in the Territorial Army. He has previously been an elected City Councillor for Newcastle, serving two terms. He has experience as a school Governor for both Primary and High Schools in the City. Now in retirement he is a Board Trustee for a Newcastle Charity supporting older people, and is involved with a Newcastle Heritage group.

‘’I’m proud to stand for re-election in Kenton Ward where I’ve been a local councillor since 2002. I’m a  governor on the city’s adult education service and a  trustee on the Kenton Park Sports Centre. I’ve been a college teacher, was born, brought up and educated in the north West of the city. I’m married to Margaret, a west end NHS Community Nurse.

Despite the challenging times we find ourselves with the cost-of-living crisis Kenton’s Labour Team continues to keep people informed, listening and working for residents all year round. We’ve prioritised the local environment by spending available cash on tackling litter, dog mess, fly-tipping and restoring green planters. With Ged and Paula, I’ve spoken up for Kenton, which is one of the most deprived wards in our city where 1 in 3 youngsters live in child poverty. That’s why I’ve supported the Welfare Rights Project, Citizens Advice and the local food bank who take food poverty. I’ve worked with the local neighbourhood police force, the EpiC team and Neighbourhood Wardens to tackle anti-social behaviour and low-level crime.

Kenton Labour is the only party to be here all year round and not just at election time. I’m asking for your vote so I can continue to work hard to make Kenton and our city a great place to live, study and work, despite the challenges we face.’’

Ged has been a local councillor since 1996. His professional career for over 30 years has been with the local fire service spending the majority of his time as an operational firefighter in or around our area. Working closely with several colleagues over the decades, one of his priorities has been and continues to be that of keeping residents informed, listen to their concerns and work for them all year round. With hard work and determination he has overcome issues in the past and is confident that with the experience he has gained, Kenton can continue to overcome any challenges that it may face in the future.

Paula has been a Labour councillor since 2016, and after 32 years working for a local authority, she took early retirement to focus on the neighbourhoods and people in the communities she represents. Currently she’s a cabinet member for Housing and Children and Families. Both areas are close to her heart as she wants families to have the best start in life, attend good schools, be healthy and cared for, and live in homes that are safe and secure. Paula wants to continue the work your local Labour team have done in supporting residents – funding the welfare service, using ward funds to support community groups, tree planting, litter picks for example, and dealing with individual concerns.

I am originally from Yorkshire and moved to Newcastle at 18 to study at the university. I have lived here for more than 25 years. Before entering local politics, I worked in the NHS as a Consultant for 15 years, taking early retirement a few years ago. I became involved with the Green Party after meeting local members and seeing their hands‑on, community‑focused approach, which challenged some of my earlier assumptions about politics...including the Greens!

I am also neurodivergent and have ADHD, which shapes how I approach problem‑solving. I tend to look at issues from different angles and have a strong sense of fairness and inclusion. I believe in recognising people’s strengths and supporting individuals to contribute in ways that work for them. I am interested in working collaboratively with residents, supporting local services, and helping address practical issues across the ward.

Over the past twenty years, I've seen the negative effects of funding cuts to our city. Public services continue to deteriorate; inequality and homelessness continue to worsen. I'm standing to challenge that approach. As a councillor, I'll focus on practical improvements informed by what local residents know is needed, from better local services and pothole repairs to waste management and the revitalisation of parks and community spaces, especially for young people. But I'll also push to change the bigger picture. We must demand investment. We have the people, the skills, and real needs that, when ignored, damage the wellbeing of our residents. We should be securing the resources to address them. By working together, we can get things done locally while challenging the austerity narrative that has left communities like ours behind.

Chris has been a University Lecturer at Newcastle and Northumbria, a Civil Servant, and more recently has worked for a local estate agent as a Property Manager. If elected, he will support the building of more private and social housing - in the right area and supported by the right infrastructure - and prioritise the principle of local homes for local people.

Grandson of a miner, retired business owner, father to two NHS doctors and one businesswoman, worked in NHS for 20 years, season ticket holder at SJP, follows Red Bulls and Durham CCC. Keen to bring business experience and financial acumen to our city council, focused on creating growth and wealth in the city to build value-for-money services that support and prioritise our constituents' needs.

Driven by Labour values, formerly Head of a planning authority, and local resident, Simon has ran his own business, taught in our local schools, and served in our armed forces. He is standing as your councillor to do what he does best – getting things done for the local community. He will fight for local bus service improvements, more Children’s and community facilities and infrastructure, Clean streets and maintained roads, footpaths and cycleways.

Liam works at Newcastle University, where his research focuses on children and young people’s mental health. With a background in youth work and experience as a parish councillor, he brings a strong local voice and a clear understanding of the challenges facing families and communities.

Gordana has lived in Kingston Park for 20 years and is familiar with local issues. She has worked to protect green spaces, for clean air in the face of increasing traffic congestion, and has helped get funding for play area improvement in Kingston Park. She has a special interest in improving public transport and interconnectivity, and is keen to bring her energy to making a real difference to the local community.

I've lived in Lemington for 30 years. Born and raised in the Outer West pit villages, my family's roots run deep. For three decades I've supported local children, families, vulnerable residents and mental health in our community. I'm standing for Reform UK because this village deserves better.

Hello, I'm Adrian, your Reform UK candidate. I've lived and worked in the North East since 2007 after 7 years service in the British Army and 2 tours. I moved to Lemington 10 years ago and proudly call it home. I want to work with our community to improve our ward and make sure your voice is heard

I'm a proud local lad raised in Blucher, with deep family roots in the Outer West pit villages. I am someone who'll support residents, stand up for local people, and fight for real improvements all year round - not just at election time.

Barry is a long time resident and Lemington ward Councillor who has supported thousands of people across Lemington ward since first elected in the early 1990’s.

Barry was involved in the delivery of Newburn Riverside Business Park, he worked very closely with the Friends of Lemington to deliver The Lemington Centre and provide continued support to them, he also worked closely with South West Denton Tenants and Residents Association to resolve problems and support tenants during the modernisation of their estate.

Barry works with local residents’ groups, community projects, schools and the local community for many years. His work included projects to improve the environment, helping to secure funding for youth services, tackling anti-social behaviour by coordinating community safety days with Northumbria Police, housing officers, and the Fire Service.

Barry commented “I am privileged to represent the people of Lemington ward as one of their local Councillors. Having lived in Lemington for most of my life, I care deeply about the area and our community. With your support, I would be honoured, to continue to represent the people of Lemington ward once again.”

Stephen is the newest councillor for Lemington ward having been elected in May 2024. Stephen is a passionate campaigner within the community, helping to organise regular litter picks, community events, and holds weekly ward walkabouts where he speaks with residents and reports any local issues. He is also very active in the local community Facebook groups keeping residents updated. He has a wealth of experience supporting residents and getting things done in the community both as a councillor and through his involvement with various local groups, churches, and charities.

Eleanor is a trustee of moving parts arts- an award-winning charity, and has helped put on an annual parade to celebrate the city (including one with a life-sized metro puppet!), as well as working in schools and community facilities. As part of her work in the community, she has previously worked with Rape Crisis Tyneside on a project.

Eleanor said: “Community is central to Lemington and therefore must be central to local government. As your councillor I would welcome any opportunities to work with community groups within Lemington ward to develop strategies to help the area to further serve the community.”

I want to raise my children in a city that has all of the public infrastructure that I grew up with. The Green Party is the only party that is committed to ending austerity and bringing back vital infrastructure into public ownership.

This is why I will be representing the Green Party in Lemington, to make sure that the streets are clean and to get youth spaces up and running. To rebuild publicly ran transport, to build community ran services and lower residents energy bills, but most of all to make hope normal again.

Newcastle has been overlooked by Westminster for far too long. I'm ready to get to work and put our city back on the map.

For too long, Newcastle has been left out of the conversation. We're taken for granted by people who expect our votes but give so little back. This must change.

I'm standing for the Greens because I believe in a fair deal for ordinary people, a sustainable future for our kids, and a party that understands that if you want a better world you have to start by talking to the people on your street, getting organised, and putting in the hard graft to get them what they need to thrive.

Everywhere that Greens have served as local councillors they've proved that they live their values - thinking globally, acting locally, and most of all turning up for their constituents.

On May 7, with your help, we can turn over a new leaf for Lemington, and for Newcastle. Vote Green.

Greg has been a leading Lib Dem councillor on Newcastle City Council since 1998.  He grew up and attended school in York before attending Newcastle University, and has lived on Tyneside since 1992. He has worked as external funding officer for a North East university, securing millions of pounds of EU funding for regional development projects in the region, and as a consultant in the planning and regeneration sector.

He has particular expertise relating to regeneration, economic development, housing, and transport, and has previously served as the Lib Dem group leader on the Tyne & Wear Integrated Transport Authority which oversaw the region’s public transport networks including bus and Metro, and as the Lib Dem lead on the LGA Urban Commission.

Greg was a Lib Dem parliamentary candidate at the 2001 and 2005 General Elections, and stood as the party’s candidate in the Sedgefield by-election in 2007. He has also acted as the party’s North East regional media co-ordinator and as a spokesman for the party on regional TV and radio.

I've lived in Jesmond and Sandyford for the last seven years. I'm doing a PhD in epilepsy research at Newcastle University alongside working as a software developer working on infrastructure to support low-emission transport planning, monitoring the impact of green infrastructure, and other urban planning analysis.

I love this city and I believe that with a Green council Newcastle could really thrive. We need local representatives that aren't afraid to make changes to improve the lives of everyone here, who will listen to the views of the people they represent, and who will work hard to find solutions to problems if they arise. If elected I promise to do my best to make this happen for the people of Manor Park.

Chris has lived in Newcastle since the 1990s and in the NE7 area since 2010. His children attended the local schools and he has been actively involved in community activities for many years. An avid supporter of Heaton Stannington FC which supports many local initiatives, Chris wants to further support the community by representing it on the City Council. Having represented the South Heaton ward from 2007 till 2016 Chris brings a wealth of experience of council operations, a financial acumen from his work as a financial planner and a support of co-operative opportunities through his membership of the Co-op Party.

Daniel is a lecturer in political philosophy at Newcastle University and spends his days trying to understand how to make our society better-run and more genuinely democratic. He has also been involved in local life, taking art classes at the community centre, and singing in the local choir. As a councillor for Manor Park, he would dedicate himself to solving residents’ problems, large and small, and to making sure the Council is responsive to the community’s concerns and attentive to its needs.

Chris works in a strategy role in local government in Tees Valley, he is a politics geek and loves running, travelling and reading detective fiction novels. He is eager to bring his skills and enthusiasm for the people of Manor Park.

I left school at 16. As a mature student, I got an engineering degree at Northumbria University. As North of Tyne Mayor, I created thousands of real living wage jobs and built affordable homes. In my 5 years as Mayor, I claimed £0 expenses.

Councillors should be shop stewards for the community in the council, not cheerleaders for the council in the community. I've been an anti-racist campaigner since the late 1980's. I'm pro-trans and LGBTQ+ rights and a White Ribbon ambassador, the campaign for men to speak out against violence against women and girls.

As Mayor, I invested £2 million into rebuilding a derelict youth centre near St James' Park. Local people now have the NUCASTLE sports centre on Diana Street. Disabled people play football. Asian elders play walking cricket on the all-weather rooftop pitch. Thousands of young people get mental health support. It brings our community together.

I have spent much of my career so far combatting the negative consequences of poor health and environmental policy. I'm standing to be a local councillor for Monument as I want to start tackling the root cause of these issues and really make a large impact on the lives of my neighbours.

The Green Party are the only party with a hopeful and forward-looking vision. We put people at the heart of policy, always ensuring that what we're doing is right for those that have put their trust in us. By combining hope, fairness, and forward thinking, we can make a real difference to everyone's lives.

I've decided to step forward because I truly believe our infrastructure is not working as it should. Too many things are falling short, and the policies meant to support people aren't always delivering.

My background is in business, management and charity work, where I've worked with people from all walks of life. This has given me a clear understanding of the challenges many in our community face.

I believe politics should bring people together, not divide us or leave anyone behind. We need practical solutions that work for everyone.
I'm standing because I care about our community and want to be a voice for positive, real change.

A fresh perspective and simple, practical ideas to the everyday challenges facing residents, businesses and workers in Monument & the city center. It's time for new voices, open conversations and honest effort to help our community thrive through real, down-to-earth local leadership that supports residents and the local enterprises that serve them.

<
I live in Newcastle with my daughter, and we feel lucky to be part of such a diverse and thriving community.

I'm used to wearing many hats and prioritising a busy workload; my work experience is in retail and hospitality management. I'm a mature student at university, a parent to a neurodiverse child, and a carer for my disabled brother.

Local services should be right for all local people, not just the few who make the most noise or have the right connections. I have autism, which for me comes with a strong sense of justice. I speak up for what is right, I don't just give up when things get tough or other voices are louder.

I'm standing as a candidate because representation is important. People deserve fairness, with services that look after them and their local area. They deserve to feel that their voice and their community matters.

I have lived across the North East all my life, and one thing has been constant, that the establishment parties are letting our conmunities down. Their career politicians fail to stand up for us against the wealthy and powerful, giving out contracts to big companies who take profit out of our city and leave us with the mess and failing services.

Something new is needed, not the false promises and scapegoating from the far right, but hope and real practical change, rooted in community. I'm standing for the Green Party to help make this happen and to speak up for my family, friends and neighbours. I want to work with you to make us proud of our city again.

I will use my experience in property management and working with small businesses, to campaign for better housing standards, spending locally, protecting our green spaces for future generations and reversing the privatisation of our vital services.

I believe every community across Newcastle upon Tyne deserves real hope for a positive future. Newbiggin Hall & Callerton may sit on the far edges of the city, but truly deserves the same commitment, investment, and attention as any other ward. The Green Party can offer exactly that.

I am committed to the Green Party's vision for affordable homes, accessible public transport that connects people to work and leisure, and neighbourhoods where everyone feels safe. I am standing here because I believe these goals are achievable for Newbiggin Hall & Callerton and because the Green Party is the only party offering real change for this community.

A vote for the Green Party on 7th May is a vote for your community, rather than a vote for division.

I've been **campaigning and doing advice work** for years. I'm councillor for Ouseburn ward and the **Council's Disability Champion,** and I've built a record of **supporting residents** against developers, landlord misconduct, and Council housing failures. I'm pushing to **reform Council debt collection** and its overuse of bailiffs and courts.
I've had enough of **politicians dodging the truth** about **crumbling services,** and I'm standing to demand the **honesty and action** residents deserve.
I joined the Green Party to **oppose unfair benefit cuts and stand publicly with Palestinians.** My husband and I have raised our children in Ouseburn since 2000. I've been a director of the local credit union for 19 years, and you'll often see me around the ward with my **guide dog, Umber.**

We’re thrilled to announce that Dr. Sarah Peters is standing as the Green Party candidate in the upcoming South Jesmond by-election on Thursday, August 14.

Sarah is a local resident with a strong record of community action. She's passionate about social justice, the climate, and making sure South Jesmond's voice is heard loud and clear in the council chamber.

I moved to Newcastle in 2019 and have lived in Ouseburn with my wife and three children since 2021.

We love being part of such a **vibrant community,** one where so many people are **looking out for each other** and caring for our local environment.

I became involved in local politics because of the experiences I've had as a parent. **Our streets and public spaces are not designed with the most vulnerable in mind,** and the council has missed so many opportunities to make moving around our city **safer, healthier, and more environmentally friendly** - these are **basic rights** that **everyone deserves.** If elected, I look forward to **working with my neighbours** and other councillors to deliver real change for Newcastle.

Robert is a lifelong resident of Newcastle and has spent years working in housing public policy, developing approaches around mortgages, renting and affordability, with a particular focus on supporting first-time buyers onto the housing ladder. He cares about keeping Ouseburn a vibrant, welcoming place as it grows, supporting new homes and businesses while protecting the community that’s already here. Robert wants regeneration to benefit existing residents, not price them out, and is committed to tackling antisocial behaviour, improving buses and safer streets, and making sure local voices are properly heard in decisions.

Ali has proudly called Newcastle his home for the past 23 years. He holds a PhD and currently works with Santander, His greatest passion has always been serving and strengthening our community. As Vice-Chair of an anti-radicalisation initiative, he has worked closely with families and young people to promote integration and help keep our youth away from extremism. Ali cares deeply about our community which is why he has decided to stand as a local councillor — to help make our neighbourhood safer, improve quality of life, break down barriers between people, and bring communities together in peace and unity. He will stand with residents, listen to them, and work tirelessly to support our community whenever needed.

Joseph is a student at Newcastle University studying Politics and International Relations specialising in Inequality studies and social policy. Joseph is a leader in his student community and wants to ensure all those living in Ouseburn are fairly represented. If elected, Joseph would dedicate himself to delivering for the people of Ouseburn and would ensure that all residents are at centre of his work. Joseph would tackle issues that matter to the local community such as housing standards, the cost of living and the environment.

Amy is a disciplined, community‑driven leader with a background in the British Army, frontline casework, and youth development. She combines practical experience with academic focus as she works toward a History degree, while serving as RSM in the Marine Training Corps and supporting young people across local schools and clubs. Her work reflects commitment, resilience, and a genuine drive to strengthen her community.

Linda is a Throckley girl born and bred, and has been a Councillor for the area for over 29 years. She is accessible and works hard to deal with the day to day issues which affect residents including housing, bin collections, grass cutting, pot holes etc, as well as the larger issues within the ward which are affecting our residents but are harder to fix. She will never stop working to make the area a better place to live

Adam is proud to have represented the Callerton & Throckley ward for the past three years, and as a local Newcastle resident he is keen to continue that work in making our area and neighbourhoods a better place. As a family man, with two young children, Adam knows how important our local area is and he is more determined than ever to help provide a good future for the next generation. Adam currently sits as a Cabinet member with responsibility for Adult Social Care, Health and Prevention.

Mark Hardy is a passionate proud Geordie and a Throckley resident. He serves as a trustee of a local charity and as a magistrate. He is standing in the City council elections because he can use his enthusiasm and experience from a 30 year career in business to make a difference to the local area and wider City.

I'm Kimberley Barrass, born and raised in the North-East before working away, in London, across the UK and in Europe. I returned home to Newcastle over ten years ago and moved to Throckley over three years ago to enjoy its fabulous location and natural surrounding beauty.

Since returning home I was disillusioned with two party politics. However, in recent times the ability of other parties to succeed and deliver has been inspiring and I joined the Green Party to contribute back to my community and country.

In particular, I believe that the positive politics of the Green Party, as stated in our manifesto, alongside my own personal experience of successful project delivery represents the best option for the Throckley, Walbottle & Newburn ward to make sure that the council delivers for our community and that our beautiful home ward is centered in all council spending and decisions.

It has never been more important to have Green voices in our communities and across the country. We need common-sense, practical solutions to the biggest challenges of our time.

As a mental health worker in a Walker-based GP practice, I see the impact of 14 years of austerity every day. I see people with mental health difficulties sat on waiting lists, unable to get the support they need. I see the stress of low wages and rising rents driving people to despair, alongside NHS staff burnt out trying to meet the needs of patients. I also see community services doing their best to provide support despite never-ending funding cuts. It is clear to me the growing mental health crisis is inextricably linked to an economy that serves the very few at the expense of the many.

Despite these challenges, we have so much to be proud of in Newcastle East and Wallsend. We have a rich industrial heritage, a long history of solidarity and our famous warmth and good humour. I’m always struck by the resilience of the people I work with and I believe that given the opportunity, our communities can thrive. 

The Green Party vows to empower communities and fix the climate crisis alongside the growing crisis of inequality. We will invest in renewable energy to improve energy security and reduce both energy bills and emissions. We will build thousands of affordable houses and insulate existing ones, to make sure that everyone can live in a safe, warm home. We will provide affordable public transport to help people stay connected, improve air quality, and leave more money in your pocket. We will bring vital services like water back into public hands to protect our shared environment. The Green Party will always place people before profit.

Vote Green for “Real Hope. Real Change"

Hi! I'm Chuck Taylor. From the first moment me and my wife visited Walker we could feel its warmth and community spirit, and that's why we've made it our home.

But after living here for only a short time it became clear it was a community that was being ignored, a community that had been taken for granted, a community that had been let down.

Then I had a knock on the door from the Green Party, asking to hear my thoughts and concerns as a local and I knew that someone was listening.

So I joined them in their work cleaning up our streets and green spaces, and knocking on doors to listen to the concerns of people round here.
Now I am determined to give those people a voice. Vote Green on May 7th, and I'll make sure that the people of Walker's voices are finally heard.

Newcastle Green Party have been drawing attention to climate change for years and the City Council’s declaration of a Climate Emergency is a step in the right direction. Yet the scale of the climate and ecological crises facing us demand more urgent action. We have until 2030 to make the changes needed to avert the setting in motion of catastrophic climate change.

We believe that now is the time for bold ideas and a change from ‘business as usual’. Newcastle Green Party would like to reimagine our city by making it more accessible, reducing inequality and raising the standard of living for everyone.

I have lived in Walker since 1987, nearly 40 years now.  My origins are in the North Wales Coal Field and I have always felt at home in Walker.

From 1973 I lived in England for work and study and I learned that all of us face the same challenges.

In 1983 I moved to Newcastle, to set up a specialist multidisciplinary Communication Aid Service for the North of England.

Working with adults, children and their families living with severe communication difficulties taught me a lot.  I learned much more about how "the system" really works, or doesn't, when I needed disability services myself and for family members in Newcastle.

While working in Newcastle, I gained valuable experience representing the interests of others.

**Some highlights:**

- Elected by the Northern Region TUC to represent Women's Interests on the North East Regional Assembly

- Regional President of MSF (now Unite Trade Union)

- Initiated and led campaigns at TUC and Labour Party Conferences against the closure of Swan Hunter Shipyard.

As a past long-standing member of **Newcastle Joint Advisory Group on Physical and Sensory Disability,** I will see that adults and children with disabilities and the elderly have access to quality services enabling participation in education, public life and decision making, ensuring privacy, dignity and respect for personal autonomy.

**The job of Councillors** is not just to kick up a fuss about pot holes, dog shit on pavements, fly tipping and HMOs springing up everywhere. They are very important because they can make our lives misery and they are the bread and butter of being a Councillor.

**But there is so much more that goes under the RADAR.**

You also need to know how your Councillors will vote on "big picture" issues that affect women, families and children living in Walker.  For example:

**Councils have a legal duty under the Equality Act 2010** to protect and uphold the rights of women as a distinct, legally recognised sex.

- For services involving

Gavin Maw has proudly lived in Newcastle Upon Tyne since 1988.

After qualifying as a dentist at Newcastle University, he worked in NHS practices across the North East before running his own successful dental practice from 1998 to 2020.

Frustrated by what he sees as over 25 years of betrayal by the political establishment, and the country’s financial, social and cultural decline.

Gavin joined the 4.07 million who voted for Reform UK in 2024's General Election and quickly became an active Reform member.

He now serves as Chairman of Newcastle’s North Branch and is
standing in the South Jesmond By-Election to bring a positive and much-needed change at a local level.

Gavin believes in common-sense politics, driven by local people’s real concerns.

Christine has lived in Walker and Walkergate area all her life, and attended a local school in Walker. Her two sons who are also local, were active members of Walker Central Football Club and Newcastle East End Football Club. Christine is a busy working mother, haven worked her entire life. She has previously worked for a local authority, and for the past 27 years for a local MP helping and supporting constituents with a variety of issues so she already has the skills to be a brilliant local councillor.

Brad has lived on the same street in Walkergate his entire life.
