He attended school in the area at St. Albans Roman Catholic Primary School, has been involved with the local Football Club Newcastle East End FC and built further ties with the community through his caseworker role for our local Member of Parliament. Working for one of our local Labour MPs has allowed him to support residents in our area with a multitude of issues, ranging from their housing or benefits, to more local issues such as anti-social behaviour or cleaning up our spaces.

I'm Margaret Montgomery. I was brought up in Wallsend with my eight brothers and sisters. It was through my family, education & community that my values were formed. Values that have developed but haven't changed since getting married, moving to Walkergate, bringing up my two children and being **involved in community** activities. **I look for the best in people,** and believe in **helping and supporting people in need.**
Since my recent retirement from teaching, I find that my grandchildren, family, friends & social connections make my life fulfilled. I believe in **empowering people in our community** to live the best lives they can live. I see my role as a councillor as being **directed by the needs of Walkergate residents.**

My name is Maria Maza. I'm originally from Spain, but I have lived in Newcastle for most of my adult life, and in Walkergate for the last 23 years. I have raised a family here. I have a background in education, home education, and the arts. Now I am a freelance photographer, doing my best work in documentary projects exploring communities coming together to celebrate. I bear witness to that togetherness, which reminds us what we can accomplish when we trust and help one another.

I am not a politician, I don't strive for power or a political career. I am stepping up to stand as your Green Party candidate in Walkergate because I want to help. I want to listen and get things done. I want us all to stop feeling fear and hate, and to start feeling hope and work for a better world together.

Abdul Rajib is a Newcastle-based accountant and Fellow Member of his professional awarding body, where he also served as Secretary. He completed two terms as Chairperson for the North East region of a charity with over 10,000 members. Abdul actively supports the community by providing free financial advice to small businesses, sponsoring cultural and sports events, presenting community TV programmes, and volunteering during COVID. He is committed to tackling anti-social behaviour, promoting a cleaner and safer environment, and improving life for all residents. Featured in British Bangladeshi Who’s Who 2018 and author of two books, Abdul believes “Positivity always wins.”

My name is Wayne Thompson, I am a civil engineer originally from Liverpool. I have been a resident of the North East since the year 2000. Since then I have fallen in love with the area and have a strong desire to improve the lives of its people.

I believe that, for far too long, we have been asked to pay increasingly large amounts for very little in return. I believe in cleaner, more affordable energy, an end to homelessness through increased social housing, publicly owned public transport and a council that works on behalf of the people it represents and not in the interests of big business. If elected I will strive for better for the people of Walkergate.

We are clear on what residents want to see here in West Fenham and Slatyford:

• Better play equipment in our play areas.
• Potholes and pavements repaired so residents can get down the street safely.
• Proper enforcement to stop flytipping.
• More council housing so that we get people off the housing list, and they can get housing locally.

We have already secured better grass cutting in the ward, helped pensioners get their heating back on, reported fly-tipping and abandoned cars. We have highlighted issues from across the ward at city council meetings and to those in charge at the Civic Centre.

But the Council is simply not doing enough to clean up Fenham and Slatyford. We want a Council which is easier for you to contact, which listens to what you say and which delivers for all of Newcastle. We want a council that focuses on value for money, which gets Newcastle moving and delivers better services.

Your Lib Dem team will deliver the **real** change that you want. Please use your **three** votes to keep your Lib Dem Team fighting for you.

• Proper enforcement to stop flytipping.

A Green Party member for over a decade, I'm standing for election because the Green's priorities of affordable housing, public transport that works and spaces for nature that we all benefit from are what Newcastle needs right now.

As a Project Manager, I'm used to working with different groups of people to get things done, skills needed to make our city better for everyone.

Vote Green on May 7th

West Fenham deserves councillors who will act on what residents are telling us and push for the practical changes people want to see. I arrived in Newcastle 25 years ago and it quickly became home. Throughout my time here, I've been driven by a passion for improving neighbourhoods and tackling inequalities at a local level.

My background in community development means I work collaboratively with people to make change happen. I've also held senior roles in large organisations and served on the boards of environmental and creative charities, giving me a strong understanding of how to navigate complex systems and get things done. I believe the Greens bring a more constructive and hopeful approach to local politics.

Residents tell us they want better maintained green spaces, reliable and affordable public transport, action on housing, and real opportunities for young people.
These are the priorities that I will fight for.

Navya has lived in Fenham for 15 years and is still fiercely proud to call it home. She is a local business owner, who is community-minded, and on first-name terms with most of the neighbourhood. She cares about clean streets, safe spaces, strong local businesses, and looking out for one another. Community isn’t a buzzword for her, it’s her everyday life.

Saeem is a civil servant by profession, but in his spare time he likes to give something back to the local community by volunteering in various activities. As a sports activist, he gets to travel to different places within the city. By doing so, he has learned a lot about the challenges that our community in West Fenham faces, from environmental issues and limited access to health and wellbeing services to traffic safety concerns and housing challenges. He is committed to delivering practical and workable solutions to the issues faced by West Fenham residents.

Connor works on the Tyne and Wear Metro, and is a trade unionist. As a trade union rep, he is used to raising concerns and issues from members and liaising with various stakeholders to get a resolution. He plans on doing exactly the same for the residents of West Fenham. Connor is standing as a Labour candidate to be a voice for those that too often are not engaged with the political process. Ordinary working people like himself will be at the heart of every discussion and decision taken by the council.

I am Humaira Khan, I am a teacher and a **DPSI qualified legal interpreter for the NHS, police and court**. I am also winner of Newcastle City Council's **Community Champion Award** for helping the NHS and coordinating between the Council and the community.

Above all, I am a **social worker** and a volunteer community worker. I believe that **"To uplift a woman is to uplift a generation".** My commitment is to ensure that **women lead with purpose and pride in a truly inclusive Green movement.**

As a woman councillor in the Green, I will do my best to facilitate and **help workers, renter, migrants, and the planet.** Together, we shall make Wingrove **cleaner and greener** for our children and community. I am standing for **hope, not hate; peace, not war.**

Please vote for me on 7th May.

I am Younes Mohammed, I am a retired business man, community worker and Chairman of Pakistan Muslim Association Bilal Jamia Mosque.

My priorities are to **reduce crime and anti social behaviour** and to improve our **local environment**. I am advocating for **better public services**, supporting residents through the **cost of living** crisis and advocating for **justice for Palestine**.

Please vote for me on 7th May.

My name is Jamal Sarwar, and I have proudly lived in the West End of Newcastle since the early 1980s. This is where I raised my family, and **where my heart and community are.**

I served for more than 10 years as Chair of the Newcastle British Bangladeshi Taxi Drivers Association, and I also worked as an interpreter with the NHS and Social Services, **helping people access the support they need.**

Now that I am semi-retired, I want to dedicate even more of my time to **serving our community.**

That is why I am standing in the May council elections. My priority is simple, to help create a better West End of Newcastle where all communities live **together in harmony**, and where we have **clean, safe, and welcoming neighbourhoods** for everyone.

Irim is a qualified solicitor, her legal skills and experience complement her work as an elected member since first elected in 2008, Irim is also a member of the councils cabinet since 2021. She currently chairs the Safe Newcastle Partnership and has positively influenced policy development across the board. She co-created the Race Charter and the Council’s Equalties Board placing emphasis on race discrimination. Irim has championed various local projects over the years and holds regular surgeries in the ward and works hard to make Wingrove a great place to live, work and study.

Joyce has represented the residents of Wingrove Ward for over 30 years and is well known and respected. She always listens to understand the issues residents feel need to be resolved and works hard to do so. She has worked with council officers and partners to improve the lives of residents. As a socialist, she believes we should help those least able to help themselves and has always stood up for that.

Rebecca is determined to make a difference to the lives of Wingrove residents and promote a more fair and equal society. By treating everyone with dignity and respect she will ensure that your voice is heard on issues that matter to you – from safer neighbourhoods, to housing, the environment and cost-of-living.
